additionalWorkerNodePools.description: Specifies the list of additional worker node pools.

annotations.description: Use annotations to attach arbitrary non-identifying metadata to objects.

autoScalerMax.description: Specifies the maximum number of virtual machines to create.

autoScalerMin.description: Specifies the minimum number of virtual machines to create.

haZones.description: Specifies whether high availability (HA) zones are supported. This setting is permanent and cannot be changed later. If HA is disabled, all resources are placed in a single, randomly selected zone. Disabled HA allows setting autoScalerMin to 0 and autoScalerMax to 1, which helps reduce costs. It is not recommended for production environments. When enabled, resources are distributed across three zones to enhance fault tolerance. Enabled HA requires setting autoScalerMin to the minimal value 3.
haZones.title: HA zones

labels.description: Use labels to identify, filter, and organize objects.

machineType.description: Specifies the type of the virtual machine. The machine type marked with “*” has limited availability and generates high cost. This setting is permanent, and you cannot change it later. To use a different machine type, you must create a new worker node pool, migrate workloads to it, and decommission the old one.

name.description: Specifies the unique name of the additional worker node pool. The name must consist of lowercase alphanumeric characters or '-', must start and end with an alphanumeric character, and can be a maximum of 15 characters in length. Do not use the name “cpu-worker-0” because it's reserved for the Kyma worker node pool.

taints.description: Specifies taints for the worker node pool. With the taints added, a node can repel sets of Pods with no matching tolerations.

administrators.description: Specifies the list of runtime administrators.
administrators.title: Administrators

auditLogAccess.description: Specifies whether Audit Log Access is enabled. Once enabled, you cannot disable it.
auditLogAccess.title: Enable Audit Log Access

autoScalerMax.description: Specifies the maximum number of virtual machines to create

autoScalerMin.description: Specifies the minimum number of virtual machines to create

machineType.description: Specifies the type of the virtual machine.

modules.description: Use default modules or provide your custom list of modules. Provide an empty custom list of modules if you don’t want any modules enabled.

channel.description: For the default modules, specifies your preferred default release channel: regular or fast. For details, see Modules.
channel.title: Default Module Channel

default.description: Check the default modules in the default modules table.
default.title: Use Default

channel.description: Specifies your preferred release channel, regular or fast, for all modules in your custom list. You can change this setting for individual modules if needed. For details, see Modules.
channel.title: Default Module Channel

list.description: Check a module technical name on this website. You can only use a module technical name once. Provide an empty custom list of modules if you don’t want any modules enabled.

channel.description: Select your preferred release channel or leave this field empty. Overrides the Default Module Channel.

_enumDisplayName.CreateAndDelete: CreateAndDelete - default module resource is created or deleted.
_enumDisplayName.Ignore: Ignore - module resource is not created.

customResourcePolicy.description: Select your preferred CustomResourcePolicy setting or leave this field empty.

name.description: Check a module technical name on this website. You can only use a module technical name once.
name.title: Name

oidc.description: OIDC configuration. The list-based configuration is recommended. The object-based configuration is provided for backward compatibility. The object-based configuration inputs are still writable, but only from the JSON view.

list.description: Specifies the list of OIDC configurations. Besides the default OIDC configuration, you can add multiple custom OIDC configurations. Leave the list empty to not use any OIDC configuration.

clientID.description: The client ID for the OpenID Connect client.

encodedJwksArray.description: The JWKS array encoded in base64. Leave empty to not use it or to remove the previously set value.

groupsClaim.description: If provided, the name of a custom OpenID Connect claim for specifying user groups.

groupsPrefix.description: If specified, causes claims mapping to group names to be prefixed with the current value. The 'oidc:' value results in groups like 'oidc:engineering' and 'oidc:marketing'. The value '-' (dash character without additional characters) can be used to disable all prefixing.

issuerURL.description: The URL of the OpenID issuer, only HTTPS scheme will be accepted.

requiredClaims.description: A list of key=value pairs that describes a required claim in the ID Token. If set, the claim is verified to be present in the ID Token with a matching value.

signingAlgs.description: A comma-separated list of allowed JOSE asymmetric signing algorithms, for example, RS256, ES256.

usernameClaim.description: The OpenID claim to use as the user name.

usernamePrefix.description: If provided, all usernames are prefixed with this value. If not provided, username claims other than 'email' are prefixed by the issuer URL to avoid clashes. To skip any prefixing, provide the value '-' (dash character without additional characters).

clientID.description: The client ID for the OpenID Connect client.

encodedJwksArray.description: The JWKS array encoded in base64. To remove the previously set value, enter the value '-' (dash character without additional characters).

groupsClaim.description: If provided, the name of a custom OpenID Connect claim for specifying user groups.

groupsPrefix.description: If specified, causes claims mapping to group names to be prefixed with the current value. The 'oidc:' value results in groups like 'oidc:engineering' and 'oidc:marketing'. The value '-' (dash character without additional characters) can be used to disable all prefixing.

issuerURL.description: The URL of the OpenID issuer, only HTTPS scheme will be accepted.

requiredClaims.description: A list of key=value pairs that describes a required claim in the ID Token. If set, the claim is verified to be present in the ID Token with a matching value. To remove the previously set claims, use the value '-' (dash character without additional characters).

signingAlgs.description: A comma-separated list of allowed JOSE asymmetric signing algorithms, for example, RS256, ES256.

usernameClaim.description: The OpenID claim to use as the user name.

usernamePrefix.description: If provided, all usernames are prefixed with this value. If not provided, username claims other than 'email' are prefixed by the issuer URL to avoid clashes. To skip any prefixing, provide the value '-' (dash character without additional characters).
